---
title: "Giorgio Maarraoui"
---

::: {.hero-panel}
::: {.hero-grid}
::: {.hero-main}
::: {.hero-copy}
## Economist | Applied Scientist

I am an economist and quantitative researcher with experience spanning **causal inference**, **policy evaluation**, **market research**, and **applied data analytics**. My work combines rigorous empirical design with large, messy real-world datasets to answer decision-relevant questions clearly and defensibly.

Alongside my PhD, I work with the **World Bank Poverty & Equity Global Practice**, where I contribute to **Commitment to Equity (CEQ)** assessments and broader fiscal-incidence analysis. Across academic and applied settings, I work on problems that involve behaviour, incentives, segmentation, distributional outcomes, and the effects of policy or market changes.

::: {.signal-row}
`Econometrics`
`Causal Inference`
`Market Research`
`Policy Evaluation`
`Data Analytics`
:::
:::

::: {.hero-aside}
### Current Roles

**University of Manchester**  
PhD in Economics  
Expected completion: May 2026

**World Bank**  
Economic Consultant, Poverty & Equity Global Practice

### Focus Areas

::: {.flat-list}
- Causal inference and quasi-experimental design
- Applied economics and decision science
- Market research and behavioural analysis
- Data analytics, segmentation, and forecasting
- Policy evaluation and distributional impact
:::

### Links

::: {.flat-list}
- [CV](cv/Giorgio_Maarraoui_CV.pdf)
- [Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=YmJMv2wAAAAJ&hl=en)
- [ORCID](https://orcid.org/0000-0002-2749-5782)
- [Teaching](teaching.qmd)
:::
:::
:::

::: {.hero-media}
![](img/portrait.jpg){width=250 .portrait}
:::
:::
:::

::: {.feature-card}
### What I Do

I work on analytical problems that require more than dashboard reporting: defining the right question, building a credible design, combining multiple datasets, and producing findings that can support real decisions. My toolkit includes **econometrics**, **causal inference**, **observational and survey data analysis**, **high-dimensional spatial and household data**, and clear communication of uncertainty, trade-offs, and impact.
:::

::: {.feature-card}
### Ongoing Applied Work

At the World Bank, I work on fiscal-incidence and equity analysis using the CEQ framework. Current projects include **forthcoming 2025 CEQ studies for Pakistan and Djibouti**, alongside earlier work on poverty, inequality, and gender in Djibouti. The work involves linking large household and policy datasets, measuring who gains and loses under alternative policy designs, and turning technical analysis into concise outputs for decision-makers.
:::

::: {.feature-card}
### Research and Writing

My research papers focus on adaptation, conflict, welfare, and policy under uncertainty, but the transferable strength is broader: framing ambiguous problems, designing credible analysis, and extracting usable insights from imperfect data. That combination is relevant well beyond academia, including roles in economics, experimentation, strategy, risk, and data-driven product or policy teams.
:::
